wins: Narrowing FY '25 adjusted EPS outlook range to $20 to $21
Implementing significant new pricing actions relating to demand and fuel surcharges
Progress on network transformation and Network 2.0 rollout
Improved service levels and reduced churn in Europe
Successful pilot of hold-to-match solution to lower last-mile costs
risks: Weaker-than-expected U.S. domestic package market demand
Pressure from international export demand surcharge tapering
Challenging industrial economy impacting B2B volumes
Potential impact from East Coast port disruptions on air freight volumes
